title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder
description:
## Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder

Given two integer arrays:
- `preorder` — the preorder traversal of a binary tree (root, left, right)
- `inorder` — the inorder traversal of the same tree (left, root, right)

Construct and return the binary tree as a level-order list.

All values in the tree are unique.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= preorder.length <= 3000`
- `preorder.length == inorder.length`
- `-3000 <= preorder[i], inorder[i] <= 3000`
- `preorder` and `inorder` consist of unique values.
- Each value of `inorder` also appears in `preorder`.

examples.explanation: Root is 3 (first in preorder). In inorder, 9 is left of 3, and [15,20,7] is right.

solutions.label: Recursive with Hash Map — O(n)
solutions.explanation: The first element of preorder is always the root. Find it in inorder — everything to its left forms the left subtree, everything to its right forms the right subtree. Use a hash map for O(1) lookups in inorder. Recursively build left and right subtrees using the corresponding slices of preorder and inorder. A shared pointer `pre_idx` advances through preorder as nodes are consumed.

solutions.label: Recursive with Slices — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Same recursive idea but uses list slicing and linear search (`.index()`) instead of a hash map. Each call creates new sub-lists, leading to O(n²) time and space in the worst case (skewed tree). The optimal solution replaces `.index()` with a hash map for O(1) lookup and uses index boundaries instead of slices to avoid copying.

solutions.label: Recursive with Hash Map — O(n)
solutions.explanation: The first preorder element is the root. Use a hash map for O(1) inorder lookups. Recursively build left and right subtrees using index boundaries. Convert the resulting tree back to a level-order list.

hints: The first element in preorder is the root. Find it in inorder — elements to its left form the left subtree, to its right the right subtree.
Use a hash map to find the root's position in inorder in O(1). Recursively build left and right subtrees.
lcEquivalent: 105. Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
